note: Supplemental feature rows from live public feeds. Merge with data/processed/evacuation_reference.json for Evo training; do not treat as labeled evacuation outcomes.

hazards.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 9:40PM PDT until June 29 at 2:00AM PDT by NWS San Diego CA
hazards.description:
* WHAT...West winds 30 to 45 mph with gusts to 65 mph, and locally
up to 70 mph, expected. Strongest winds on Saturday afternoon and
evening.

* WHERE...San Gorgonio Pass near Banning.

* WHEN...Until 2 AM PDT Monday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest winds expected Saturday evening.
hazards.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.
hazards.area_desc: San Gorgonio Pass Near Banning

hazards.headline: Red Flag Warning issued June 27 at 8:16PM PDT until June 27 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Hanford CA
hazards.description:
* AFFECTED AREA...Fire weather zone 298. Fire weather zone 299.
Fire weather zone 595.

* WIND...70 TO 80 PERCENT PROBABILITY OF GUSTS EXCEEDING 55 MPH
IN EASTERN KERN COUNTY. 80 TO 90 PERCENT PROBABILITY OF GUSTS
EXCEEDING 30 MPH AT LAKE ISABELLA.

* HUMIDITY...EASTERN KERN COUNTY MINIMUM RELATIVE HUMIDITY
BETWEEN 12-20 PERCENT. LAKE ISABELLA MINIMUM RELATIVE
HUMIDITY BETWEEN 25-35 PERCENT.

* THUNDERSTORMS...NO THUNDERSTORM THREAT

* IMPACTS...any fires that develop will likely spread rapidly.
Outdoor burning is not recommended.
hazards.instruction:
A Red Flag Warning means that critical fire weather conditions
are either occurring now, or will shortly. A combination of
strong winds, low relative humidity, and warm temperatures can
contribute to extreme fire behavior.
hazards.area_desc: Indian Wells Valley; Kern County Desert; Lake Isabella

hazards.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 7:14PM PDT until June 27 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Las Vegas NV
hazards.description:
* WHAT...South winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts up to 40 mph are
expected.

* WHERE...In Arizona, portions of Mohave County, including Lake
Havasu and Fort Mohave. In California, portions of San Bernardino
County, including the Colorado River Valley.

* WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT / 11 PM MST this evening.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Extreme caution is advised while out on area
lakes. Areas of blowing dust are possible as well.
hazards.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for
high-profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

Secure outdoor objects.

Strong winds and rough waves on area lakes will create hazardous
conditions for small craft.
hazards.area_desc: Lake Havasu and Fort Mohave; San Bernardino County-Upper Colorado River Valley

hazards.headline: Red Flag Warning issued June 27 at 7:09PM PDT until June 27 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Las Vegas NV
hazards.description:
...RED FLAG WARNING NOW IN EFFECT UNTIL 8 PM MST SUNDAY FOR
STRONG WINDS AND LOW HUMIDITY FOR NORTHWESTERN ARIZONA...

...RED FLAG WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT FOR STRONG WINDS AND LOW
HUMIDITY FOR MUCH OF SOUTHEASTERN CALIFORNIA AND SOUTHERN NEVADA
FROM THIS MORNING THROUGH SATURDAY EVENING...

* TIMING...Through this evening.

* WIND...Southwest winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts to 40 mph.

* HUMIDITY...5 to 15 percent.

* IMPACTS...Any fires that develop will likely spread rapidly.
Outdoor burning is not recommended.
hazards.instruction:
A Red Flag Warning means that critical fire weather conditions
are either occurring now...or will shortly. A combination of
strong winds...low relative humidity...and warm temperatures can
contribute to extreme fire behavior.

hazards.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 1:17PM MST until June 28 at 11:00PM MST by NWS Phoenix AZ
hazards.description:
* WHAT...West winds 25 to 35 mph with gusts up to 55 mph.

* WHERE...Imperial County Southwest.

* WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Difficult driving conditions, especially for larger
vehicles traveling along roads with crosswinds. Light, unsecured
objects may become airborne.
hazards.instruction:
A Wind Advisory means that sustained wind speeds of between 30 and 40
mph are expected, or wind gusts of between 40 and 58 mph. Winds this
strong can make driving difficult, especially for high profile
vehicles.
hazards.area_desc: Imperial County Southwest

hazards.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 1:17PM MST until June 27 at 11:00PM MST by NWS Phoenix AZ
hazards.description:
* WHAT...Southwest winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts up to 45 mph.

* WHERE...In Arizona, Central La Paz and Parker Valley. In
California, Chuckwalla Valley, Imperial Valley, and Palo Verde
Valley.

* WHEN...Until 11 PM MST /11 PM PDT/ this evening.

* IMPACTS...Difficult driving conditions, especially for larger
vehicles traveling along roads with crosswinds. Light, unsecured
objects may become airborne.
hazards.instruction:
A Wind Advisory means that sustained wind speeds of between 30 and 40
mph are expected, or wind gusts of between 40 and 58 mph. Winds this
strong can make driving difficult, especially for high profile
vehicles.

In addition, strong winds over desert areas could result in briefly
lowered visibilities to well under a mile at times in blowing dust or
blowing sand. Use extra caution.
hazards.area_desc: Parker Valley; Central La Paz; Chuckwalla Mountains; Imperial County Southeast; Imperial County West; Imperial Valley; Palo Verde Valley; Chuckwalla Valley

hazards.headline: Beach Hazards Statement issued June 27 at 1:05PM PDT until July 1 at 5:00AM PDT by NWS San Francisco CA
hazards.description:
* WHAT...An increased risk of sneaker waves and strong rip
currents expected.

* WHERE...Pacific Coast beaches.

* WHEN...Through Wednesday morning.

* IMPACTS...Dangerous swimming and surfing conditions and
localized beach erosion can be expected. Sneaker waves can
sweep across the shoreline without warning, pulling people
into the sea from rocks, jetties and beaches.
hazards.instruction:
Stay off of jetties, piers, rocks, and other waterside
infrastructure. Remain out of the water to avoid hazardous surf
and NEVER turn your back on the ocean. Monitor local weather,
surf and tide forecasts at www.weather.gov/mtr.
hazards.area_desc: San Francisco; Coastal North Bay Including Point Reyes National Seashore; San Francisco Peninsula Coast; Northern Monterey Bay; Southern Monterey Bay and Big Sur Coast

hazards.headline: Air Quality Alert issued June 27 at 6:37AM PDT by NWS San Diego CA
hazards.description:
* WHAT...The South Coast AQMD has issued an air quality alert due to
harmful levels of particle pollution from windblown dust. Visit
https://www.aqmd.gov/alertinfo to check current and forecasted air
quality levels.

* WHERE...in the Western Coachella Valley near Cathedral City.
Levels of particle pollution can vary by time and location depending
on emissions and local weather conditions.

* WHEN...until Sunday at 8:00 AM.

* IMPACTS... Particle pollution can get deep into the lungs and
cause serious health problems such as asthma attacks, heart and lung
disease symptoms, and increased risk of lung infections. Everyone
can be affected, but sensitive groups such as people with lung or
heart disease, older adults, people who are pregnant, children, and
those who spend a lot of time outdoors are at greater risk.
hazards.instruction:
To protect your health, check air quality levels and act as needed:

* When Air Quality Index (AQI) levels are "Unhealthy for
Sensitive Groups" (orange), sensitive groups as specified
above should limit extended or intense outdoor activity.
* When AQI is "Unhealthy" (red), everyone may experience health
impacts. Sensitive groups should avoid extended time outdoors.
* When AQI is "Very Unhealthy" (purple), sensitive groups should
avoid all outdoor physical activity. Everyone else should
avoid extended or intense outdoor activity.
* When AQI is "Hazardous" (maroon), everyone should avoid all
outdoor physical activity.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

To help keep indoor air clean when air quality is poor:

* Keep windows and doors closed
* Run your air conditioner and/or an air purifier
* Do not use whole house fans or swamp coolers that bring in
outside air if you have other methods to stay cool
* Avoid other sources of pollution such as fireplaces, candles,
incense, grilling, and gasoline-powered lawn and garden
equipment

To help minimize outdoor particle pollution levels:

* Carpool, telecommute, reduce trips, or take public
transportation
* Slow down if driving on dirt roads
* Stabilize loose soils

For the latest air quality forecasts and information, visit the
website at aqmd.gov.
hazards.area_desc: Coachella Valley

hazards.headline: Lake Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 12:33AM PDT until June 27 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Reno NV
hazards.description:
* WHAT...Southwest winds 15 to 25 mph with gusts up to 40 mph for
Stampede Reservoir, Lake Davis, Antelope Lake, and Eagle Lake.

* WHERE...Lassen-Eastern Plumas-Eastern Sierra Counties.

* WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT this evening.

* IMPACTS...Small boats, kayaks and paddle boards will be prone to
capsizing and should remain off lake waters until conditions
improve.
hazards.instruction:
Check lake conditions before heading out and be prepared for a
sudden increase in winds and wave heights. Consider postponing
boating activities on the lake until a day with less wind.
hazards.area_desc: Lassen-Eastern Plumas-Eastern Sierra Counties

hazards.headline: Lake Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 12:33AM PDT until June 27 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Reno NV

* WHERE...Greater Lake Tahoe Area.

* WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT this evening.

hazards.area_desc: Greater Lake Tahoe Area; Greater Lake Tahoe Area

raw_hazards_sample.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 9:40PM PDT until June 29 at 2:00AM PDT by NWS San Diego CA
raw_hazards_sample.description:
* WHAT...West winds 30 to 45 mph with gusts to 65 mph, and locally
up to 70 mph, expected. Strongest winds on Saturday afternoon and
evening.

* WHERE...San Gorgonio Pass near Banning.

* WHEN...Until 2 AM PDT Monday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest winds expected Saturday evening.
raw_hazards_sample.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

raw_hazards_sample.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 9:40PM PDT until June 28 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS San Diego CA
raw_hazards_sample.description:
* WHAT...West winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts 45 to 55 mph, and
locally up to 60 mph, expected.

* WHERE...San Diego County Deserts.

* WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest winds expected Saturday evening.
raw_hazards_sample.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

raw_hazards_sample.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 9:40PM PDT until June 28 at 5:00AM PDT by NWS San Diego CA
raw_hazards_sample.description:
* WHAT...Southwest winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts 40 to 50 mph
expected.

* WHERE...Apple and Lucerne Valleys.

* WHEN...Until 5 AM PDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strongest winds expected Saturday evening.
raw_hazards_sample.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

raw_hazards_sample.headline: Lake Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 8:46PM PDT until June 28 at 2:00AM PDT by NWS Hanford CA
raw_hazards_sample.description:
* WHAT...West winds 15 to 20 mph with gusts up to 35 mph.

* WHERE...Los Banos - Dos Palos and West Side Mountains north of 198.

* WHEN...Until 2 AM PDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Strong winds and rough waves on area lakes will create
hazardous conditions for small craft.
raw_hazards_sample.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.
raw_hazards_sample.area_desc: West Side Mountains north of 198; Los Banos - Dos Palos

raw_hazards_sample.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 8:46PM PDT until June 28 at 5:00AM PDT by NWS Hanford CA
raw_hazards_sample.description:
* WHAT...Southwest winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts up to 50 mph.

* WHERE...Indian Wells Valley and Mojave Desert.

* WHEN...Until 5 AM PDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
raw_hazards_sample.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

raw_hazards_sample.headline: Red Flag Warning issued June 27 at 8:16PM PDT until June 27 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Hanford CA
raw_hazards_sample.description:
* AFFECTED AREA...Fire weather zone 298. Fire weather zone 299.
Fire weather zone 595.

* WIND...70 TO 80 PERCENT PROBABILITY OF GUSTS EXCEEDING 55 MPH
IN EASTERN KERN COUNTY. 80 TO 90 PERCENT PROBABILITY OF GUSTS
EXCEEDING 30 MPH AT LAKE ISABELLA.

* HUMIDITY...EASTERN KERN COUNTY MINIMUM RELATIVE HUMIDITY
BETWEEN 12-20 PERCENT. LAKE ISABELLA MINIMUM RELATIVE
HUMIDITY BETWEEN 25-35 PERCENT.

* THUNDERSTORMS...NO THUNDERSTORM THREAT

* IMPACTS...any fires that develop will likely spread rapidly.
Outdoor burning is not recommended.
raw_hazards_sample.instruction:
A Red Flag Warning means that critical fire weather conditions
are either occurring now, or will shortly. A combination of
strong winds, low relative humidity, and warm temperatures can
contribute to extreme fire behavior.
raw_hazards_sample.area_desc: Indian Wells Valley; Kern County Desert; Lake Isabella

raw_hazards_sample.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 7:14PM PDT until June 27 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Las Vegas NV
raw_hazards_sample.description:
* WHAT...South winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts up to 40 mph are
expected.

* WHERE...In Arizona, portions of Mohave County, including Lake
Havasu and Fort Mohave. In California, portions of San Bernardino
County, including the Colorado River Valley.

* WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT / 11 PM MST this evening.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Extreme caution is advised while out on area
lakes. Areas of blowing dust are possible as well.
raw_hazards_sample.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for
high-profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

Secure outdoor objects.

Strong winds and rough waves on area lakes will create hazardous
conditions for small craft.
raw_hazards_sample.area_desc: Lake Havasu and Fort Mohave; San Bernardino County-Upper Colorado River Valley

raw_hazards_sample.headline: Wind Advisory issued June 27 at 7:14PM PDT until June 28 at 5:00AM PDT by NWS Las Vegas NV
raw_hazards_sample.description:
* WHAT...West winds 30 to 40 mph with gusts up to 55 mph.

* WHERE...Portions of Inyo County, including the southern Owens
Valley.

* WHEN...Until 5 AM PDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strong cross winds could impact portions of US
395 south of Olancha. Areas of blowing dust are possible as well.
raw_hazards_sample.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for
high-profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

Secure outdoor objects.

raw_hazards_sample.headline: Red Flag Warning issued June 27 at 7:09PM PDT until June 27 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Las Vegas NV
raw_hazards_sample.description:
...RED FLAG WARNING NOW IN EFFECT UNTIL 8 PM MST SUNDAY FOR
STRONG WINDS AND LOW HUMIDITY FOR NORTHWESTERN ARIZONA...

...RED FLAG WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT FOR STRONG WINDS AND LOW
HUMIDITY FOR MUCH OF SOUTHEASTERN CALIFORNIA AND SOUTHERN NEVADA
FROM THIS MORNING THROUGH SATURDAY EVENING...

* TIMING...Through this evening.

* WIND...Southwest winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts to 40 mph.

* HUMIDITY...5 to 15 percent.

* IMPACTS...Any fires that develop will likely spread rapidly.
Outdoor burning is not recommended.
raw_hazards_sample.instruction:
A Red Flag Warning means that critical fire weather conditions
are either occurring now...or will shortly. A combination of
strong winds...low relative humidity...and warm temperatures can
contribute to extreme fire behavior.
raw_hazards_sample.area_desc: Lake Mead NRA/Colorado River-AZ side; Death Valley National Park; Mojave Desert Including Morongo Valley/Yucca Valley; Lower Colorado River Valley; Esmeralda and Nye County Deserts/CNC Dispatch; Lincoln County/Ely Dispatch; Nye County Deserts/Las Vegas Dispatch; Sheep Range; Spring Mountains; Clark and SW Lincoln County Deserts/Las Vegas Dispatch; Lake Mead NRA/Colorado River-NV side
